scope: Kingston, Gananoque/Thousand Islands, Brockville/Leeds-Grenville, Prescott, South Dundas, South Stormont, Cornwall/SDG
Relevant public online events and regional civic dates
metadata.inclusion_rule: Publicly accessible upcoming events dated on or after 2026-07-21, plus official bulk calendars and feeds that require automated harvesting.
metadata.exclusion_rule: Expired, clearly private, spam/predatory, false, and exact duplicate records.
metadata.qualification_rule: Uncertain details remain visible with qualification notes; source-calendar rows identify high-volume official sources rather than inventing individual records.

methodology: Compared every researched event against the 244 future Audit 14 records using normalized title similarity, exact start date, and city.
Preferred official municipal, university, library, museum, festival, theatre, and open-data sources.
Recorded both verified individual events and source-calendar records for official calendars whose full volume requires automated harvesting.
Kept original source URLs and research-reference IDs with every row for later verification and implementation.
Flagged source registry coverage as exact URL, same domain only, possible name match, or new source.
limitations: This is a discovery snapshot as of 2026-07-21. Event calendars change continuously.

Large calendars such as Toronto Public Library and Ville de Montréal contain thousands of listings; their complete contents should be harvested through adapters or open-data feeds rather than manually copied.
Social-only, unindexed, newly announced, ticketing-platform-only, and private events can remain outside a web research snapshot.
Source-calendar records are implementation targets and should not render as public events.
